author: Star Atlas
content: I've had a friend, someone who I've known in the space for a super long time. They messaged asking if I was currently in a conference call this lovely scammer @RobtechGit said I was in/part of. I am not. I will never been in a random conference, I will always share on my socials if Im ever doing a podcast, talkshow etc.   Don't Download links from people on the internet asking you to join a call,  Do not link any wallets or anything like that  Do not sign anything Don't interact with random drops   DO always be super careful in these situations and research  I don't know what this person is attempting to do but I am not part of this, I am not in this conference and I doubt some of the others showing as being there are actually them, they most likely took our profile pictures to lure people in.

author: Star Atlas
content: I love it when a plan comes together.  Star Atlas is the world. @ZinkSVM is the agent execution layer. Together, they create the first crypto-native environment where AI agents can safely act as economic participants.  That is much stronger than “AI in a game.”  The stack:  Star Atlas = persistent economy Ships, land, fleets, resources, markets, factions, governance, player labor.,  AI agents = operators Fleet managers, market makers, faction coordinators, onboarding copilots, autonomous NPC/org actors, creator/business agents.,  http://z.ink / zProfile = permissioned execution, Agents need to act without holding private keys or asking for 500 signatures. zProfile lets users delegate bounded authority: asset limits, spending caps, actions, revocation, identity/reputation.  The investment thesis Crypto’s killer AI use case is not “chatbots with tokens.” It is permissioned autonomous economic activity.

author: Star Atlas
content: SCAM ALERT - "Annual Access Pass" We want to bring awareness to a new scam email some users have been receiving.  This email congratulates the user for staying loyal to Star Atlas.  This email offers the "Annual Access Pass."  This email is not from the Star Atlas team or anyone affiliated with the Star Atlas team.  If you receive an email similar or the same as the one shown in the images here, BLOCK AND DELETE.  If you aren't sure about an email from us, always check the sender address, or join us on http://discord.gg/staratlas to speak to a mod or admin.

author: Star Atlas
content: Every time Star Atlas visits a campus, it feels like we’re opening the universe a little wider for more people to step inside. The SBU Open House was no different 🩷  Campus Starbase Round 3 had such a warm and curious crowd. Students kept stopping by to explore UE5 and HoloSim with us, and their reactions were so genuine. Some stayed to chat, some asked questions, and some simply wanted to imagine what this universe could become.  The tees and snacks disappeared quickly, but what stayed with me was the energy.  More pilots are coming. More stories are beginning. This is how @staratlas universe grows 🚀🩷  #StarAtlasThailand #StarAtlas #Web3Gaming @WeAreStarAtlas @staratlastv  @romeguild @Vanguard_DAC @MUD_Empire @QuimeraSA_ @at_mwagner @Santiacc_ @DominicVain @SuperPhoenixDAO

author: Star Atlas
content: Lot of posts out there today about Toly’s support of @ZinkSVM. And I get it. Feels like we’re leaving @solana. We’re not. We’ve deployed >25 onchain programs for Star Atlas. I would put our blockchain team against the best of any project on Solana. Many of those programs will continue to run on Solana. Our game systems need to shift.  I had calls with Toly and Raj in Q4 2020. I was convinced then that tech is the most scalable option. I still am. That’s why we picked SVM. Solana has seen incredible success over the past 5 years. That success has introduced some issues for us, which we intend to solve with http://z.ink.   But we’re extremely appreciative of the support that we’ve received over the years. And for many applications, Solana truly is the best.

author: Star Atlas
content: My best estimate is that we're very rapidly approaching the point of maximum capitulation on web3 gaming (and alts more broadly). Yes, it's been sad seeing the many studio closures over the year. I empathize with the losses people have experienced. And the loss in confidence in this new paradigm.  But I still believe gaming represents one of, if not the best use case for blockchain tech and crypto. There's a natural synergy with game economies, and real value ownership and capture. The quality teams (like Star Atlas) that have endured have not been resting on their laurels waiting for better market conditions. They've been building. They've been following their dream, and preparing themselves for releases, campaigns, and genuine sustainable growth. This consolidation to quality is going to lead to unfathomable success for those that have made it to this point.  Speaking for Star Atlas, I have incredible conviction that we are sitting on a powder keg. Our development velocity has only accelerated with time. We are positioned with game experiences catering to an insanely wide audience. We have a balanced and rewarding game economy that's been running smoothly for years. We have tokenomics with some of the best fundamentals I've seen in any web3 game. The smallest spark is going to ignite the engines, not to the moon, but to the next galaxy.  Until then, we keep grinding.

content: It's awesome to see that one of the crypto gaming OGs is still building  Every gameplay video @staratlas drops looks amazing  It takes time to build a good game The difference between web2 and web3 games in this regard is that web2 games build behind the scenes and web3 games build in public  We have a front row seat for all the setbacks, challenges, wins, and all in between  It's time to start supporting all games on all chains that have been building for a while  Star Atlas has our attention. Let's see what they come up with once they launch. Until then we'll be following the updates🔥🫡

author: Star Atlas
content: The latest Star Atlas Quarterly Economic Report is LIVE! 📊  This quarter, our newly released Fleet Rental program generated over 1,000,000 ATLAS per days for lenders! 🤯  🔗 | Download and read the full report: https://staratlas.com/newsroom/economic-reports https://pbs.twimg.com/media/GnZN1D0aoAAZMTx.jpg

author: Star Atlas
content: Other Highlights Include:   🔹 The ONI faction earned 12% more ATLAS this quarter than MUD and USTUR, dethroning MUD as the most productive despite the large wealth disparity.   🔹 The Golden Carnival created approximately 158 million ATLAS worth of resource burn this quarter, while simultaneously stimulating the economy through ship prize drops.   🔹 Star Atlas GDP contracted by 44% this quarter amid volatile markets, but showed signs of recovery toward the end of the period with the release of the Fleet Rental program.   🔹 Active fleets grew by 15% on average post fleet rental release, and ship classes such as small and x-small saw considerable increases in demand.   🔹 Local market volume share surpassed 30% excluding transactions occurring at the CSS. Large discrepancies in price between markets provided opportunity for arbitrage.

author: Star Atlas
content: For those who haven’t noticed this yet @staratlas put out an economic dashboard on the governance site linked below. You can use this to check out local markets and see where all the volume is at and what items are in demand. Click on a Starbase hot spot and see all the demand there or click on a specific item and it’ll show you where the items are bought/sold the most at.   Team up this tool with @staratlasmaps where you can see the order book for each item at each starbase to see prices.   Mining and shipping back to css? Try seeing if there is demand for your resources at the hot spots in each faction or perhaps try to make a market for it. You can save on fuel from making less trips or increase your production by venturing out to the MRZ.   One example, hydrogen at MRZ 20 in Oni is in high demand and is selling for nearly the same as hydro at the CSS and hydrogen there has 50% more richness.   https://govern.staratlas.com/ecosystem-dashboard

author: Star Atlas
content: SAGE Labs earners, a new update is live!   We’ve made some changes to Faction Infrastructure Contracts. Check out the patch notes below! 👇  🔹 Each faction now gets 1 new recipe. The new recipe replaces Frameworks with Crystal Lattice and reduces the recipe value from SDUs.  🔹 FICs will now only refill when all sectors have no remaining buy orders. If one sector runs out but others still have orders, the empty sector will not be refilled until the rest are also depleted.  🔹 Each faction’s two FIC recipes will now spawn in 2 locations each (4 total per faction). These locations will be randomly re-assigned every Thursday at 17:00:00 UTC.  🔗: https://based.staratlas.com/
